findings.title: Small-phase + RolePrompts: cross-file findings silently dropped
findings.summary: `AutoPhaseDiffPrompt` (internal/agent/prompts.go:165, :190) instructs reviewers to skip cross-file concerns because an arch reviewer is assumed, but `parsePhases("small")` (review.go:820–840) creates only diff reviewers over the full diff with no arch reviewer. With `RolePrompts=true` on small/auto-phase, cross-file findings are silently suppressed across all reviewers.
messages: internal/agent/prompts.go:165: AutoPhaseDiffPrompt assumes an arch reviewer has the full diff, but parsePhases("small") creates only `diff` reviewers over the full diff; cross-file issues can be missed.
internal/agent/prompts.go:190: AutoPhaseDiffRefFilePrompt repeats the same false subset/arch-reviewer assumption for ref-file mode.

[must] AutoPhaseDiffPrompt says 'arch reviewer has the full diff … Skip cross-file architectural concerns', but parsePhases small uses single bucket {Phase:"diff", ReviewerCount:N} with no arch reviewer; small + RolePrompts=true silently excludes cross-file design bugs.

internal/agent/diff_review.go:30: RolePrompts switches any non-empty phase to role-specific prompts, so `--phase small` diff reviewers get AutoPhaseDiffPrompt and are told to skip cross-file concerns even though no arch reviewer runs.

findings.title: CLI flag precedence: --no-role-prompts=false silently overrides ACR_ROLE_PROMPTS=true
findings.summary: main.go:441 marks `RolePromptsSet=true` whenever either `--role-prompts` or `--no-role-prompts` was changed, and main.go:495 computes `RolePrompts: rolePrompts && !noRolePrompts`. Explicit `--no-role-prompts=false` therefore reports the flag as set with value `false` and overrides `ACR_ROLE_PROMPTS=true`, breaking the flags > env > yaml > defaults contract.
messages: [must] main.go:441 RolePromptsSet uses `Changed("role-prompts") || Changed("no-role-prompts")` and main.go:495 computes `RolePrompts: rolePrompts && !noRolePrompts`; `--no-role-prompts=false` sets RolePromptsSet=true, value=false, silently overriding ACR_ROLE_PROMPTS=true.
Fix: gate Changed on `noRolePrompts` independently, or compose as `(rolePrompts || envSetTrue) && !noRolePromptsExplicit`.

findings.title: Ref-file prompt regression: AutoPhase ref-file variants drop 'Use the Read tool' directive
findings.summary: Existing ref-file prompts (`DefaultClaudeRefFilePrompt` prompts.go:66–69, `DefaultGeminiRefFilePrompt` L90–93, `DefaultCodexRefFilePrompt` L140–143) all explicitly tell the agent to read the diff file. New `AutoPhaseDiffRefFilePrompt` / `AutoPhaseArchRefFilePrompt` only say 'Review the code changes in %s', breaking the implicit ref-file contract and risking empty responses on the large-diff fallback path.
messages: [must] DefaultClaudeRefFilePrompt(prompts.go:66–69), DefaultGeminiRefFilePrompt(L90–93), DefaultCodexRefFilePrompt(L140–143) all say 'Use the Read tool to examine it.'; new AutoPhaseDiffRefFilePrompt / AutoPhaseArchRefFilePrompt only say 'Review the code changes in %s' with no read instruction.
Risk: ref-file path (large-diff fallback) — agent never fetches diff body, may return empty response. Restore explicit 'Use the Read tool to examine it.'

info.title: Reviewer process notes (no actionable findings)
info.summary: Reviewers documented their reading approach: scope limited to real source files, verifying RolePrompts plumbing from cmd/acr through runner into agent, confirming legacy arch ref-file prompt was intentionally fixed by tests, and limiting impact to large-diff/ref-file + new flag enabled paths.
messages: RolePrompts config resolution exists; next check cmd/acr building internal/runner.Config.
runner value pass-through confirmed at cmd/acr/review.go:352-365; ReviewConfig branches on Phase and RolePrompts.
code-review skill: read current files instead of relying on diff alone; review-only, no file changes.
Look for 'config resolved but unused' gaps in setting resolution and reviewer execution path.
Confirmed RolePrompts flows from runner to agent.ReviewConfig; next check prompt output format vs parser/summary contract.
Diff centers on role_prompts setting resolution and phase prompt selection; tracing flag/env/config precedence and Phase + RolePrompts reaching the reviewer in auto-phase.
Legacy arch prompt ref-file side intentionally not updated (locked by tests); confined real-impact check to large-diff/ref-file + new flag enabled paths.

findings.title: Small-phase role prompts issue appears in both arch and diff review
findings.summary: The arch review and two diff groups independently identify that diff prompts tell reviewers to skip cross-file concerns because an arch reviewer exists, while small-phase execution has no arch reviewer. The same pattern spans prompt definitions and reviewer execution/config wiring, so the severity should be treated as blocking rather than isolated advisory diff findings.

findings.title: CLI flag precedence bug was missed by responsible diff group
findings.summary: The arch review reports a blocking flag/env precedence bug in cmd/acr/main.go, but the diff group covering cmd/acr/main.go did not report a matching finding. This is a coverage gap between the full-diff arch pass and the file-scoped diff pass.

findings.title: Ref-file prompt read-instruction regression was not covered by diff review
findings.summary: The arch review reports that new ref-file role prompts omit the explicit instruction to read the diff file. The diff group covering internal/agent/prompts.go reported a different prompt-contract issue but did not cover this ref-file read-instruction regression, leaving a blocking arch concern without file-scoped confirmation.

findings.title: Phase validation concern lacks file-scoped coverage
findings.summary: The arch review flags silent fallback for unknown phase values in internal/agent/diff_review.go. The diff group covering that file did not report the validation issue, so the arch concern remains unaddressed by the succeeding diff pass.

findings.title: Single-phase + RolePrompts cross-file blind spot confirmed across all three diff groups
findings.summary: g01 (id=17), g02 (id=6), and arch (id=8) independently identified that AutoPhaseDiffPrompt instructs reviewers to skip cross-file concerns under the false premise that an arch reviewer is running, but parsePhases('small') has no arch reviewer. Three independent group viewpoints converging on the same defect strongly confirms this is a real, multi-surface regression and warrants blocking severity (matching arch's classification). g01 and g02 marked it advisory only because their per-group view did not see the absence of an arch reviewer in the small-phase code path; the cross-group context promotes it.

findings.title: CLI flag precedence bug missed by g01 despite main.go being in scope
findings.summary: The arch reviewer flagged a blocking trust-boundary violation at cmd/acr/main.go:441 / :495 where `--no-role-prompts=false` silently overrides ACR_ROLE_PROMPTS=true, breaking the documented flag>env>yaml>defaults precedence. main.go is part of g01's target_files, but g01 produced only meta-commentary on this file and did not surface the precedence defect. This is a coverage gap: the per-file group reviewer should have caught a defect in its own scope, and the issue was only saved by the arch reviewer's full-diff view.

findings.title: Ref-file prompt read-directive regression missed by g02 despite prompts.go being in scope
findings.summary: Arch finding id=10 (blocking) shows AutoPhaseDiffRefFilePrompt / AutoPhaseArchRefFilePrompt drop the explicit 'Read the file contents' directive that all three legacy ref-file prompts (Claude/Gemini/Codex) carry, risking empty responses on the large-diff fallback path. prompts.go and prompts_test.go are both in g02's target_files; g02's id=6 noted the subset/arch-reviewer issue in the same prompts but did not flag the missing read instruction. This is a coverage gap in g02's per-file scope that only the full-diff arch reviewer caught.

findings.title: Missed CLI flag trust boundary violation
findings.summary: The arch group identified a blocking defect in cmd/acr/main.go where a CLI flag silently overwrites an environment variable, but this was not reported by the g01 group responsible for reviewing the file.

findings.title: Missed ref-file prompt regression
findings.summary: The arch group flagged a blocking regression where read instructions are missing from ref-file prompts. This was not caught by the g02 group reviewing internal/agent/prompts.go.

findings.title: Missed phase validation issue
findings.summary: The arch group noted a missing validation for Phase values in internal/agent/diff_review.go, which was not addressed by the g01 group.

findings.title: Missed domain model layering issue
findings.summary: The arch group raised an architectural concern about mixing presentation state (RolePrompts) into the ReviewConfig domain type in internal/agent/config.go, which the g01 group did not report.

findings.title: Cross-group structural debt in config layer
findings.summary: The arch group identified structural debt requiring coordinated mechanical edits across configuration layers and flag parsing. This issue spans files distributed across g01, g02, and g03, making it inherently difficult for any single diff group to assess.
